title: with_key! silences drop via implicit id mutation
description: key.rs with_key! macro sets key.id = KeyId::ERASED before calling key_evict to prevent the Drop impl from printing a leak warning. This is implicit state: a reader must understand both the macro and the Drop impl to know why the assignment exists. Suggested rewrite: replace the id-zeroing trick with core::mem::ManuallyDrop::new(key) so the intent is explicit — the key is not being dropped normally.
